description_long: This object is a detailed red rose flower with multiple layers of velvety petals, each petal exhibiting subtle gradients of red and pink hues. The petals are arranged in a tight, overlapping spiral, creating a lush, full bloom with a naturalistic appearance. The outer petals are slightly curled and broader, while the inner petals are more tightly packed and form a pronounced central whorl. There is a hint of green at the base, suggesting the presence of a stem, though it is mostly obscured by the petals. The surface of the petals appears soft and matte, with gentle undulations and naturalistic texturing. This flower would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping.
description: A lush, red rose flower with layered, spiraled petals and a soft, velvety appearance. The bloom is full and naturalistic, with subtle color gradients and a hint of green at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full, open face of the rose with densely layered petals and a clear central whorl.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the bloom and the overlapping, curled edges of the petals.
description_view_2: Rear angled view revealing the outermost petals and a glimpse of the green base, with the flower's profile visible.
description_view_3: Oblique view showing the layered structure and the transition from the outer to inner petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral whorl, with overlapping layers radiating outward from a central point. The arrangement is radial and exhibits a high degree of symmetry, typical of many rose species.
